plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Sell the weak semiconductor holding (SMH).
Increase exposure to income (QQQI) and broad index (QQQ).
Reduce concentration in SPY to meet limits.
Add a high-quality energy name (OXY) for diversification.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a larger cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan aims for lower, steadier returns around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leverage and diversifying away from concentrated tech.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and sell others (SPAL).
Sell the weak semiconductor holding (SMH).
Increase exposure to strong tech (CRWD) and broad index (QQQ).
Reduce concentration in SPY to meet limits.
Add a quality semiconductor name (TXN) for diversification.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction, aiming for steady gains.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly returns. It reduces leverage and concentration while still participating in quality tech growth.

plan: Increase exposure to high-conviction leveraged ETF (SOXL).
Add a strong tech performer (CRWD) to concentrate gains.
Reduce broad index holdings (SPY, QQQ) to increase concentration.
Add an inverse ETF (SOXS) for potential downside protection.
Add a high-scoring scanner setup (MSFU) for aggressive growth.
This plan maximizes upside potential but carries significantly higher drawdown risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets higher returns around 4% monthly by leaning into leverage and concentration. Expect amplified gains and much larger potential losses.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and volatile tech/semis.
Maximize allocation to income-focused ETF (QQQI).
Increase allocation to broad index (SPY) for stability.
Add a stable dividend-paying energy stock (OXY).
This plan prioritizes consistent monthly income and capital preservation.
It aims for low volatility and reliable distributions over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets about 1% monthly return through distributions. It minimizes risk and volatility by holding income-generating assets and broad market exposure.
